subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/EFF, World Bank, IDB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Salvadoran official (BCR) + Salvadoran independent press (El Faro, partly in exile).
A dramatic gang-violence collapse delivering genuine physical safety, set against a hollowing-out of the rule of law; the country is dollarized and walked back Bitcoin legal-tender status in 2025.

Standout tension: a huge crime/safety turnaround vs eroding rule of law and a multi-year state of exception with mass detention. Scored as distinct, both-large effects rather than netted. Source-lean: significant Anglophone/multilateral; in-exile domestic press may understate repression.

economic.notes: Dollar-anchored stability and an IMF anchor, against high debt and remittance dependence.

fiscal_state.notes: High debt ~85-89% GDP but a 2025 IMF program restoring access and consolidating toward ~80% - high debt offset by credible adjustment. (IMF EFF 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Dollarized - zero monetary autonomy but imported stability; Bitcoin legal-tender status repealed in 2025. (IMF; BCR)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: It uses the US dollar, so no local-currency devaluation/FX risk. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Around 1% - among the region's lowest, dollar-anchored. (BCR; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Low NPLs and adequate capital, deemed sound; remittance-funded. (IMF Art IV)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: ~4% growth, tourism boosted by the security turnaround, and textiles/nearshoring, but remittances ~27% of GDP create US-immigration-policy fragility. (World Bank; BCR)

institutional.notes: Centralized, comparatively effective delivery, against an eroded rule of law and a declining press.

rule_of_law.notes: Court-packing enabled re-election and a 2025 indefinite-reelection amendment; the state of exception suspends due process - strongly negative despite popularity. (V-Dem; HRW; WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Official data is broadly usable and IMF-engaged, with some opacity. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Centralized and comparatively effective at delivery. (IMF; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A steep RSF decline, with the leading investigative outlet partly in exile and journalist surveillance. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Popular near-term stability built on suspended constitutional norms, with mass detention the cost.

political_stability.notes: A 2024 landslide re-election and durable popularity, but built on suspended constitutional norms and indefinite reelection - a long-run legitimacy risk. (V-Dem; HRW)

civil_liberties.notes: A multi-year state of exception with tens of thousands detained without due process and in-custody deaths reported. (HRW World Report 2026; WOLA)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited inflows; it accepted US deportees into its mega-prison, with abuse allegations. (HRW; CNN)

social_cohesion.notes: The gang-violence collapse is hugely popular and unifying, but on a fear-based underpinning. (WOLA; HRW)

demographics.notes: Young-ish but with a heavy emigration legacy and remittance dependence. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A close, transactional US ally with an open economy and a suppressed internal conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A close, transactional US ally (a deportation/mega-prison partnership) - Western-aligned but personalised. (CNN; PBS)

conflict_involvement.notes: No interstate war, with the internal gang conflict effectively suppressed. (CRS)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open, dollarized, not sanctioned, with free capital flows. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: A dramatic crime-rate collapse, against seismic/storm exposure.

climate.notes: Seismic/volcanic, hurricanes, and dry-corridor drought, with most of the country hazard-exposed. (World Bank GFDRR)

infrastructure.notes: Improving on security-enabled private investment. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Homicides collapsed to ~1/100k from among the world's highest - a genuine, dramatic gain, at a heavy rights cost. (BCR; HRW)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against high seismic/climate exposure. (GFDRR)

summary: A country delivering tangible safety and macro-stability dividends while hollowing out the rule of law - the central tension a reader must weigh. The gang-violence collapse is real and dramatic (homicides fell from among the world's highest to ~1/100k), inflation is low under dollarization, and a 2025 IMF program anchors a high-debt budget. The cost is a multi-year state of exception with tens of thousands detained without due process, court-packing that enabled indefinite re-election, and a press driven partly into exile. Scored as distinct, both-large effects rather than netted away.
